title: Cross-Functional Product Review - Q3 Feature Prioritization
transcript_text:
[Priya Sharma] (00:00:10): Thanks for joining everyone. We need to finalize the Q3 feature list today. The board wants our top 5 priorities by Friday.
[Sarah Chen] (00:00:28): From engineering's side, we're pushing for the real-time collaboration feature. It's been requested by 40% of our enterprise clients.
[Priya Sharma] (00:00:45): That's a big lift though. Tom, what's the infrastructure requirement?
[Tom Wilson] (00:01:02): We'd need to set up WebSocket infrastructure and upgrade our message broker. I'd estimate 3 weeks for infra alone, then another 4 weeks for the feature build.
[Maria Lopez] (00:01:20): The frontend for real-time collab is also complex. We'd need operational transforms or CRDTs. That's not trivial.
[John Park] (00:01:38): I've been researching CRDT libraries. Yjs looks promising - it could cut our development time by 60%. I can prototype something by next week.
[Priya Sharma] (00:01:55): Great, let's do the prototype. But we also need to address the mobile app performance issues. Customer complaints are up 200% this quarter.
[Sarah Chen] (00:02:12): That's critical. We need someone to audit the mobile app performance - profiling, memory leaks, the whole thing. It's been on the backlog for months.
[Priya Sharma] (00:02:30): Who can own that? It's cross-cutting - needs frontend, backend, and DevOps involvement.
[Tom Wilson] (00:02:45): I can handle the infrastructure monitoring side. But someone needs to lead the overall audit.
[Maria Lopez] (00:02:58): I could do it but I'm already overcommitted with the dashboard and the collab feature frontend.
[Sarah Chen] (00:03:15): Let's figure out the ownership offline. We can't leave this unassigned though. Someone needs to own this by end of day.
[Priya Sharma] (00:03:32): Agreed. Third priority - the analytics dashboard revamp. Sales team says the current one is losing us deals. They need real-time metrics, custom reports, and export functionality.
[John Park] (00:03:50): The backend for analytics is solid. It's mostly a frontend job. We might want to bring in a contract designer for the UX.
[Priya Sharma] (00:04:05): Budget approved for a contractor. Maria, can you write the requirements doc for the analytics dashboard by Monday?
[Maria Lopez] (00:04:18): That's doable. I'll base it on the sales team feedback from last month's survey.
[Priya Sharma] (00:04:35): Perfect. Fourth item - GDPR compliance updates. Legal is breathing down our necks. We need the data deletion pipeline and consent management UI done by end of Q3. Non-negotiable.
[Tom Wilson] (00:04:55): The data deletion pipeline is mostly done. Maybe 2 more weeks to productionize it. The consent UI is the bigger unknown.
[Sarah Chen] (00:05:10): Let's prioritize GDPR above analytics if push comes to shove. Compliance deadlines don't move.
[Priya Sharma] (00:05:25): Agreed. So our ranked priorities: 1) Mobile performance audit, 2) GDPR compliance, 3) Real-time collaboration prototype, 4) Analytics dashboard revamp. The fifth slot - any nominations?
[John Park] (00:05:45): API versioning. We've been pushing breaking changes without proper versioning. Enterprise clients are complaining.
[Sarah Chen] (00:05:58): Good call. Let's add API versioning as number 5. Priya, can you draft the priority document and circulate for sign-off by Thursday?
[Priya Sharma] (00:06:12): Will do. Let's reconvene Friday to finalize before the board presentation.
